coverage_note: Layer 3 (motion RAOs). Real named-vessel/named-field RAO datasets are almost never public — operators treat hull hydrodynamic databases (AQWA/WAMIT .lis, OrcaFlex vessel-type RAOs) as proprietary. The honest, citable answer for this layer is the published BENCHMARK / model-test community datasets, which is what these records capture. Most are floating-offshore-wind floaters (OC3 spar, OC4/OC5 DeepCwind semisub) and academic seakeeping hull forms (S175, KCS, DTC, Wigley) — directly transferable RAO methodology to FPSO/FPU/drillship/FSRU hulls, but only the DeepCwind semisub and OTRC FPSO are floating-production-type geometries. Several key facts (exact period/frequency ranges, full 6-DOF coverage, heading sets, exact downloadable file formats) live inside paywalled journal articles (ScienceDirect, Springer, T&F) or conference proceedings I could see only as abstracts/search summaries; those are flagged as "gap". OrcaWave L01 default-vessel PDF is a public brochure-grade worked example but is a generic vessel, not a named real unit. NO numeric RAO ordinates are asserted here — RAO curves are figure/table data inside the cited reports, not transcribable to a single value; per-DOF amplitudes are therefore "gap" with the dataset pointer given instead. Radii of gyration / mass properties for these models are in the definition documents (cited where a public PDF exists, e.g. OC5 definition doc) or "gap". No fabricated numbers.

verdict.summary: Spot-checked 5 headline records against the web; all primary facts verified and on-topic. OC5 DeepCwind (MARIN model 9697, 1:50, 2013 MARIN basin test, Robertson/Wendt OC5 Phase II) confirmed. OC3-Hywind correctly attributed to NREL/Jonkman, Statoil Hywind concept, 5MW spar. OTRC turret-moored FPSO (1:60, tanker-based, truncated mooring, Kim/Tahar, Ocean Engineering S002980180500051X) confirmed exactly. BSEE TAP-406 'FPSO Roll Motions' (Texas A&M OTRC, bilge-keel/roll-damping, 2-D viscous focus) confirmed; cited 406ab.pdf is a real BSEE file. OrcaWave L01 'Default vessel' correctly described as a public Orcina worked example recreating the generic OrcaFlex default-vessel hydrodynamic dataset, NOT a named real unit. Physical descriptions are all sane for the vessel classes (1:50 three-column ring-pontoon semisub; deep-draft cylindrical spar with long >20s heave/pitch natural periods; tanker FPSO roll near roll natural period in beam/quartering seas). The dataset is appropriately conservative: it asserts NO numeric RAO ordinates (all per-DOF amplitudes, period/frequency ranges, headings, radii of gyration, and mass properties are explicitly marked 'gap'), so there are no hard numbers exposed to fabrication. The honest coverage_note correctly states real named-vessel/named-field proprietary RAO databases are essentially never public and that these are benchmark/model-test community datasets, most being FOWT floaters rather than floating-production geometries. One genuine defect found: a citation label/URL mismatch on the OC3-Hywind record (see flagged_records) — but the cited URL still resolves to a real, on-topic NREL OC3 document, so this is a mislabel, not invented data. No fabrication.

flagged_records.name: OC3-Hywind spar-buoy (5 MW FOWT spar)
flagged_records.issue: Citation label/URL mismatch. The source is labeled 'Definition of the Floating System for Phase IV of OC3 (NREL/TP-500-47535)' but the URL given is https://docs.nrel.gov/docs/fy11osti/48191.pdf, which is actually NREL/TP-5000-48191 'Offshore Code Comparison Collaboration (OC3) for IEA Wind Task 23' — a different report. The real TP-500-47535 definition doc lives at docs.nrel.gov/docs/fy10osti/47535.pdf. URL is still a real, on-topic OC3 NREL document, so this is a mislabel (radii_of_gyration/mass_properties are marked gap anyway, so no false numbers leak), but the citation pointer should be corrected to fy10osti/47535.pdf.

records.name: OC4 / DeepCwind semisubmersible (5 MW FOWT floater)
records.owner_operator: Benchmark — DeepCwind consortium / IEA Wind Task 30 (model test by Technip/MARIN)

fields.geometry_type: three-column ring-pontoon semisubmersible with central tower column
fields.dof_covered: surge, heave, pitch primarily reported (3-DOF); full 6-DOF measured in test, RAOs most often published for surge/heave/pitch
fields.headings: gap — head-sea (0/180 deg) regular-wave cases most commonly published; full heading set: gap
fields.period_frequency_range: gap — regular-wave RAO cases span platform natural periods (surge/heave/pitch resonances) through wave-frequency band; exact tabulated range inside paywalled comparison papers
fields.radii_of_gyration: gap — published in OC4 system definition (NREL/TP-5000-60601); not transcribed here
fields.mass_properties: gap — defined in OC4 definition document

citations.source: Systematic comparisons among OpenFAST, Charm3D-FAST simulations and DeepCWind model test for 5 MW OC4 semisubmersible offshore wind turbine (ResearchGate listing)
Wave-Current Interaction Effects on the OC4 DeepCwind Semi-Submersible (J. Mar. Sci. Eng. 12(9):1509)
